name: Donald J. Trump
subject: Let’s have breakfast
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
Special opportunity for you. |
---
| | | | | | | | [](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f130da95757c407e6d3db9ffb07c7aae43e27c3a9068504a29357ec33f28c7dfed6e979051ceb3ebbe16e04acde5cf8a5929)
---

Friend,

I want you to enter for the chance to meet me for a breakfast I'll be hosting in Florida.

This is the very FIRST opportunity to win breakfast with me for the 2020 re-elect campaign, and as one of our top supporters, I wanted to make sure you got the invite first.

**If you win, we 'll cover your flight, hotel stay, and the meal. You can even bring a friend or family member and we'll all take a picture together so that we can all remember this moment forever.**

---
| _All it takes is any contribution of at least $3 before March 4th at 11:59 PM to be automatically entered for this once-in-a-lifetime chance ...
_
**[BREAKFAST WITH PRESIDENT TRUMP](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f130059cdc2a14799aae3539b6dad0bcb321e2b099835f1e639e2360454c4a8200d98047a09f0535170a2a7afa7cce6189a6)** **SUPPORTER: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f1309ca48120394ece062407c753f7fca28def3d2c4475ff66b01bad0377275377c6fb8bee2f1fb9610892c9cfea7ea80029)** **ENTRY:****PENDING**
**DEADLINE: March 4th at 11:59 PM** ************************************[ **CONTRIBUTE $3**](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f130b16826f0e5c78e7dfab19b29c2c3e343d5acbaa0ae39614e2a541ca3145e7f96714fc20f920f770b49418309cb5a9c0a)************************************
** [CONTRIBUTE $20](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf2f18e605a5753264f5d0c05681ce0fda99ef3d034514907f855a0631d227331fbdab04a794b61c2e68df54aeb788031eb)** ** [CONTRIBUTE $15](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf28170336628df0f8be1506d31d5e128a84c38ece54d3064a06521f52698cf72855b3d9af6c5b299d700bdbceffe5ccee1)** ** [CONTRIBUTE $10](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf2eb61db2394de90cd4a86d81a735f10dfada5ac10856b6dc97228e370ba313256dffa1d5385c6bed54d895d63f3eaaf88)** ** [CONTRIBUTE $5](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf274f0766604eee5f7e6a7418b567de11ce74cc6911e3e23295a72ffda4a9938d9b51d017094783edb519fee169d32a4c5)** ** [CONTRIBUTE OTHER AMOUNT](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf25a4af4b747f1e6442430bb8fffcfdd481a680ce4e91d234bbb018a0106499365d1df8d094c496f4fba4d6690f9b21868)**
This is the greatest part about being president.

**I love getting to meet the people who truly are the heart and soul of our beautiful nation. You are my motivation to keep fighting to Make America Great Again!**

I hope we get to meet in Florida.

**[Please contribute at least $3 to be automatically entered to win.](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf2ce7d538377d80619cc8b54ae27d45ea55f5ec01d3bbfa7c7cdc2464e40c28a83c46ef759472473e4e11509d016b99815)**

Thank you,
[](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf2edf1f55ff7de594e07392a81964714b3025f3215122ddf1b6ef2bcf7f620d95f248ce903e322623b18f152ad7b464715)
Donald J. Trump
President of the United States of America

[CONTRIBUTE $3](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=ab3fefa3881a1bf28ab6e8e4a3e05b81c8be5615f392d02cbf245cb0220c0abb088f8c89bd76d86e653eb68af7d0c166fb1f5221efce8912)
---

Contributions to the Trump Make America Great Again Committee are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. **NO PURCHASE, PAYMENT, CONTRIBUTION, OR TEXT MESSAGE NECESSARY TO ENTER OR WIN.** Contributing or sending a text message will not improve your chances of winning. Void where prohibited. You may enter by contributing to Trump Make America Great Again by clicking [here. ](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f13062b2cd2d36a2f03032cfe6a2c2b934f6578792915b41a03820d38d948f3680c7948a6526361b7d163f14018cc3123e49) Alternatively, you may enter without contributing by clicking [here ](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f130581dc9675f031dd54f50783ab5fddaa33399aa76068e6f182dd372c8c9744e25a4795f7933be2501245b6bf789096986) or by texting “FEBBREAKFAST” to 88022 & 80810 for one (1) entry. By sending a text message from your mobile phone , you are providing your written consent to receive calls and SMS/MMS messages, including autodialed and automated calls and texts, to that number from each of the participating committees in the Trump Make America Great Again Committee, Donald J. Trump for President Inc. and the Republican National Committee. Text message and data rates may apply. Consent is not required as a condition of entry. Text STOP to 88022 and 80810 to opt-out. Entries must be received between Feb 23, 2019, at 12:00 a.m. Eastern Time and ends on March 4, 2019, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time. Odds of winning depend on the number of eligible entries received. One (1) winners will receive round-trip transportation and accommodations and tickets to a breakfast event featuring President Donald J. Trump (approximate retail value $3000). The Promotion is open only to U.S. citizens, or lawful permanent U.S. residents who are legal residents (green card holders) of the 50 United States, Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia and are at least 18 years of age (or the age of majority under applicable law). Winner must be 18 or older (or of majority under applicable law) and meet other requirements as described in the Official Rules. Promotion subject to Official Rules and additional restrictions on eligibility may apply. Visit [here](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f13015d86cca018c36e7182f5f0a67751d1960d162d6457d8f01c3b2a3f396b2116b9c512ad72727fbca1eb0ebd143b22ca2) for full details and Official Rules. Sponsor: Trump Make America Great Again Committee, 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor, Beverly, MA 01915.
Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J. Trump for President, Inc. and the Republican National Committee. You are receiving this email at dpwillis67@gmail.com
Trump Make America Great Again Committee, 138 Conant Street, 2nd Floor, Beverly, MA 01915

We believe this is an important way to reach our grassroots supporters with the most up-to-date information regarding the efforts of the Trump Make America Great Again Committee and President Trump, and we're glad you're on our team. It's because of grassroots supporters like you that we will Make America Great Again, and we appreciate your support. Thank you for all that you do!

[Privacy Policy](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=959a465131b2f130c1d532c563ab809b48fb87c0eb4680f614fdff9c8ba5616b1bd16696fbdb64fd390e3d4de6d3231ec7e97b5a57a99777) | [Unsubscribe](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/unsub_center.aspx?qs=9b3e05efd8bd05fce3e522e295a2d40a3d05e468a3ed42502522125d7d26118b43a8ba8bae8d52c5c81db97a1a1c9f4b55712473b342510b431d847cda066240) | [Update Profile](http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/profile_center.aspx?qs=9b3e05efd8bd05fccf6ebf77cd5e8ea547e65639e0dc256c7cee526d9c287fb89ff88812566e3ff7b55b3bd5e7a940ffdb072ca34b53d76c1310bd87c7bbf0cd)
---

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Democrats' top pick-up opportunity
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
In just a moment, we're going to ask you to make a donation to Democrat Mark Kelly's Senate campaign in Arizona. Give us a moment to explain why.
**Arizona is a top pick-up opportunity for Democrats, and it 's an absolute must-win if we're going to take back the Senate.** Mark, a former astronaut and current gun safety champion, is the perfect leader for Arizona and just the person to stand up to the worst parts of Trump's agenda.
This race is going to be close. Trump won here by a healthy margin, and corporate special interests are going to do everything they can to keep this seat in Republican hands.
[**Mark can win, but he 's counting on your help to do it. Will you please give $3 or more right now so that Mark can flip this red seat blue and take back a Democratic Senate?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f1072f/3611308517/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
_If you 've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will automatically be split between Mark Kelly and Maggie Hassan._
**[Click to donate $3.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f10728/3611308517/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVVGeWNtRjVRU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjMifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=3")**
**[Click to donate $25.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f10729/3611308517/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVVGeWNtRjVRaUI5ZlE9PSI6IjI1In0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=25")**
**[Click to donate $50.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f1072a/3611308517/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVVGeWNtRjVReUI5ZlE9PSI6IjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=50")**
**[Or click here to donate another amount.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f1072f/3611308517/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")**
If Mark's going to succeed, he needs a strong grassroots team with him every step of the way. Since you helped Maggie Hassan win by just over 1,000 votes, there's no doubt about it: **Mark can 't win -- and Democrats can't take back the Senate -- without you.**
[**Please be part of the team that 's going to flip this seat and take back the Senate by giving $3 or more to Mark's campaign right now.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f1072f/3611308517/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")
Thank you,
Team Maggie

> [Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc155/2a380d31/3a84ffd7/f1072f/3611308517/VEsB/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE5MDIyM19NV0g0TUsyX1JPIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjMsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20190223_MWH4MK2_RO&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Trump's attack on reproductive rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
We have to speak out--now.
| | |
---
| | BREAKING: Trump administration rule would make it **ILLEGAL** for health providers to refer patients to clinics that provide abortions--including Planned Parenthood.

**Stop Trump 's attack on our reproductive rights!**
---
| | [SIGN THE PETITION](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/title-x?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2827-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg-title-x")
---
Peter,
Trump just launched one of his most aggressive and dangerous attacks on reproductive rights and health care. His domestic "gag rule" would cut Title X funding from Planned Parenthood and any health care provider that offers safe and legal abortions--or even refers a patient to a place where they can get an abortion.
This dangerous, ideological crusade endangers millions of people who rely on women's health centers as their only option for health care of any kind. This is about reproductive justice--but it's also about our basic right to decide what's best for our bodies and health.
We won't stop Trump's attacks on reproductive rights unless we all speak out together, and fast. We need 15,000 signatures against this new rule by midnight.
[**Will you speak out now? Sign the petition right now to stop Trump 's vicious attack on our reproductive rights.**](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/title-x?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2827-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg-title-x")
This is now or never. Republicans have been launching these attacks for decades, going to extreme lengths to defund Planned Parenthood, restrict abortion access, put impossible demands on providers and create an echo chamber of dangerous rhetoric around a safe, legal medical procedure.
If we're not willing to fight for autonomy over our bodies, for our health and our safety, what are we willing to fight for? Because abortion access is health care. It's safe, it's normal and it's a constitutional right. So when it's under attack, we need to stand up. Speak out. And we need to do it right now:
[**Please, before our midnight deadline, sign our petition to stop Trump 's attack on our reproductive rights.**](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/title-x?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2827-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fact.kirstengillibrand.com%2Fkg-title-x")
Thank you for refusing to back down from even the toughest fights,
Kirsten
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976a6a/2ae112ef/105a4f2f/357acb4b/2417371278/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://gillibrand.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") | | Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
---
Contributions or gifts to Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee are not tax-deductible. Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee does not accept contributions from federal lobbyists, corporate and/or trade association PACs, or "for profit" business entities, including but not limited to sole proprietorships, partnerships, and LLCs.

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Rewind to 1907
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
body:
The proudest achievement in El Paso history occurred in 1966 [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1001&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ]when the
Texas Western College Miners defeated the Kentucky Wildcats in the Men’s
NCAA Basketball Championship Game. It’s one of the great Cinderella
stories in sports history — a team of unlikely heroes who captured the
country’s imagination and changed the course of collegiate athletics
forever.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1002&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ][IMG]
Nevil Shed supporting Willie Worsley

It was an amazing team, one of the best that ever played. But in addition
to Don Haskins’ gifts as a coach and the genius of Bobby Joe Hill and
David Lattin on the court, their victory was in part the result of a
larger struggle to ensure the dignity of every man and woman in this
country.

Rewind to 1907.

In that year a young man named Lawrence Nixon was practicing medicine in
Cameron, Texas. Given the extreme, organized brutality towards African
Americans in Texas and the former Confederacy, and the many obstacles
placed in his path, it was an achievement of his persistence and
determination that he was able to earn an education at all — first at
Wiley and then medical school in Nashville before returning to Texas to
practice medicine.

But he knew that his achievement and persistence in no way guaranteed his
safety or his rights. In a reminder of the extreme danger that African
Americans faced in Texas, in November of that same year, 500 members of
the Cameron community stormed the county jail and lynched an African
American man named Alex Johnson.

Concerned by Johnson’s public murder and the number of lynchings occurring
across central and east Texas, and perhaps fearing for his own life, Nixon
moved west to El Paso where he became involved with civil rights work in
addition to his medical career.

In 1914 he helped organize the first chapter of the NAACP in the state of
Texas. And in 1924, after Texas passed a law barring African Americans
from participating in the all-important Democratic primary (in a state
where the Republican party existed in name only), Dr. Nixon took his poll
tax into the nearest polling location on election day knowing full well he
would be refused the right to vote. Turned away on the basis of his race,
he immediately mounted a challenge to the all-white primary — taking his
case to the U.S. Supreme Court, twice.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1003&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ][IMG]
Lawrence Aaron Nixon and his wife Drusilla

Ultimately, he won.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1004&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ]In 1944, 20 years after his first unsuccessful effort to vote, Dr.
Nixon walked into the same polling location that had turned him away in
1924 and cast his vote as a Texan, as an American, as a man.

Perhaps Thelma White was thinking about Dr. Nixon in 1955. She had just
graduated from the all-black Douglass high school, and though she knew
about the segregation clause in the Texas constitution, she nonetheless
attempted to enroll at Texas Western College in El Paso. Denied entry
based on the color of her skin, she enlisted the help of the NAACP and
attorney Thurgood Marshall and took her case to the federal bench of R.E.
Thomason.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1005&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ][IMG]
Thelma White

Judge Thomason, perhaps persuaded both by the arguments of White’s legal
team and by the extraordinary courage she demonstrated in defying legal
segregation, issued a ruling that not only integrated Texas Western
College but forever ended Texas’ segregation in public higher education.

This victory was quickly followed that same year by the El Paso school
district becoming the first ISD in the state of Texas to integrate its
public K-12 schools.

Then in 1957, El Paso achieved the distinction of becoming the first major
American city to be led by a Mexican-American mayor when Raymond Telles
was elected. He overcame decades of discrimination and in the process
ensured that all El Pasoans knew that they could serve in positions of
trust and power.

That was followed by El Paso city alderman Bert Williams passing one of
the first ordinances in the former Confederacy to bar segregation in
places of public accommodation after his friend Nolan Richardson was
refused service at the Oasis restaurant in 1962 on the basis of his skin
color.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1006&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ][IMG]
Bert Williams and Nolan Richardson

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16846?t=1007&akid=24950%2E444211%2EY0eM2J ]From Nixon to White to Telles to Williams, El Paso was helping to lead
the country in ensuring that we actually lived the idea that “all men are
created equal.” That there was justice for all.

And so, back to the 1966 Miners.

In the years leading up to ‘66, as Coach Haskins was building the team
that would win the National Championship, he was often faced with
skepticism from African American prospects who wanted to play for him but
didn’t want to live in a city where they’d have to sit at the back of the
bus, or drink from a separate water fountain or be treated differently
based on the color of their skin.

Thanks to the civil rights pioneers who had made their stand in El Paso,
he was able to tell them that this was a city that would welcome their
genius and their talent without respect to their race. El Paso would see
them and treat them as Americans.

With greats like Nolan Richardson and Jim “Bad News” Barnes, Texas Western
became a destination for talent from across the country. By 1966, Haskins
had the best team in the country, which happened to proudly start five
African Americans on the court.

Against the odds, against a powerhouse like Kentucky, against the
entrenched racism of the country and collegiate athletics (which had never
seen an all-black starting five in a championship game), Texas
Western — which had only twelve years before admitted its first African
American student — won the national championship, bringing glory, honor
and distinction to El Paso, to Texas (where to this day, El Paso remains
the only city in Texas to produce a men’s championship team) and to this
country. That team changed college athletics forever — speeding
integration in sports and allowing talent to thrive throughout the
country.

Though he died two weeks before the championship game was played, Lawrence
Nixon was in some way responsible for the victory that took place. As were
Thelma White, Bert Williams, Nolan Richardson and so many more. It was a
triumph not just of athletics and coaching, but one also of opportunity,
civil rights and democracy.

The 1966 victory is a reminder that our greatest achievements flow from
ensuring the greatest opportunity for all. It is a reminder of what can
happen when we confront injustice where we find it. It can speed the
country’s journey towards equality and justice. And it can have an impact
far beyond the life of the hero who makes her stand

It’s a great reminder given the times we live in and the injustices we see
today in America. We need equity in education where we must confront and
change a school to prison pipeline where students of color are five times
more likely to be suspended than their white peers. We must address an
inequity in health care, where a maternal mortality crisis impacts black
mothers at two to three times the rate of white mothers. We must make
institutional changes to our criminal justice system where we see arrest
and sentencing disparities that help explain why one in four black
children have had a parent in the criminal justice system, compared to
just four percent of white children. We must ensure the economy really
works for all, to address unconscionable wealth and income disparities
that allow access to opportunity for some over others. We must work
towards solutions that make housing, transportation, the workforce, and
higher education more equitable.

And, as Dr. Nixon would remind us — to get any of this done, we need to
make sure everyone has access to the ballot box. End racial and ethnic
gerrymandering, stop voter I.D. laws that seek to suppress voters of color
and make sure everyone can add their voice and their vote to this great
democracy.

Black History Month, El Paso’s history, and the civil rights heroes of our
communities remind us that all of this is possible but only if all of us
make it happen. It’s the courage of people like Nixon, White, Richardson,
Telles and Williams who can bring us out of our complacency, awaken our
conscience and our duty to eachother, and ultimately force people in
positions of public trust to make the important decisions on our behalf.

Courage makes victory possible.

- Beto

[ ][IMG] Nevil Shed supporting Willie Worsley It was an amazing team, one of the best that ever played. But in addition to Don Haskins’ gifts as a coach and the genius of Bobby Joe Hill and David Lattin on the court, their victory was in part the result of a larger struggle to ensure the dignity of every man and woman in this country. Rewind to 1907. In that year a young man named Lawrence Nixon was practicing medicine in Cameron, Texas. subject: It could happen ANY SECOND
body:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=613%2E150750%2EufjeiW)

This is it!
We are just a little bit away from being able to say more than 1,000,000 people have added their name to support Bernie’s run for president.
But before that happens, we wanted to give YOU a chance to be the one who reaches that number for our campaign.
**Add your name here for your chance:**
[**https://act.berniesanders.com/go/1-million**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_bern_message/?source=em190224-1-full-120&t=3&akid=613%2E150750%2EufjeiW)
Bernie is going to call number 1 million. Hopefully it’ll be you.
All our best,
Team Bernie

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to, [unsubscribe using this link](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=613%2E150750%2EufjeiW). But we hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=613_150750_ufjeiW&akid=613%2E150750%2EufjeiW).
